extracted.headquarters: 14 George Street, Budd Lake, New Jersey 07828
extracted.brandBackground: Soccer Stars is a Delaware LLC formed January 7, 2022, offering developmental soccer training and physical fitness programs for children ages 1–18 under the trademarks "Soccer Stars," "Super Soccer Stars," and "Soccer Stars United," with franchising begun in August 2022 under parent Super Sports Holdings, LLC (d/b/a Youth Athletes United).
extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise delivers structured developmental soccer and fitness curricula to children at schools, parks, and community venues — a youth enrichment/education service model.
extracted.staffingRationale: Franchisees are expected to operate from home, hire part-time coaches per session, and personally manage the business — a semi-absentee/small-crew model with no brick-and-mortar location requirement.

leadership.name: Adam Geisler
leadership.background: CEO of Soccer Stars, LLC since formation (Jan 2022) and simultaneously CEO of Amazing Athletes Franchise Systems, TGA Franchise Systems, and Little Rookies Baseball.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads the entire Youth Athletes United franchise portfolio, giving Soccer Stars access to a multi-brand franchise infrastructure.

leadership.name: John Erlandson
leadership.background: President of Soccer Stars since formation (Jan 2022) and concurrently president of Amazing Athletes, JumpBunch, Little Rookies Baseball, and TGA Franchise Systems.
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary franchisee contact and operational head across the YAU brand family.

leadership.name: Khalil A. Haddad
leadership.background: CFO of Soccer Stars since formation and CFO of TGA Franchise Systems, LMS Franchise Systems, Little Rookies Baseball, and JumpBunch.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees financial reporting and controls across all YAU brands.

leadership.name: Annie Spaulding
leadership.background: COO of Soccer Stars since January 2024, previously VP of Operations at Super Soccer Stars LLC (2018–2022) and Amazing Athletes.
leadership.whyItMatters: Drives day-to-day franchise operations and system standards.

leadership.name: Carmen Bellavia
leadership.background: CTO of Soccer Stars since formation and COO/CTO of Amazing Athletes, TGA, JumpBunch, and Little Rookies Baseball.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages the Back Office Platform and all technology infrastructure franchisees depend on.

leadership.name: Bob Gibson
leadership.background: President of Soccer Stars since January 2023, previously VP of Operations at Super Soccer Stars LLC (2019–2023).
leadership.whyItMatters: Conducts and supervises the franchisee training program.

item19.notes: Item 19 covers January 1–December 31, 2025 for 'Reporting Soccer Stars Outlets' (open at least 1 year, same owner all year). Affiliate-owned outlets (4) reported separately from franchised outlets (55 owners). Data sourced from Royalty Reporting Sheets; not independently audited. Averages are per-owner, not per-unit. Some owners operate multiple units.

cohorts.label: Affiliate-Owned Outlets — Average P&L (4 outlets)
cohorts.basis: Average of 4 affiliate-owned outlets for FY2025; figures in thousands per Table 1 (Operating Revenue $1,681K + Other Revenue $13K = $1,693K total revenue average per outlet).

cohorts.label: Affiliate-Owned Outlets — Net Income After Royalties (4 outlets)
cohorts.basis: Average Net Income After Royalties per affiliate outlet per Table 1 FY2025 P&L ($202K, 11.9% net margin).

cohorts.label: All Affiliate Outlets — NY (Gross Revenue)
cohorts.basis: Table 2: NY affiliate outlet gross revenue FY2025.

cohorts.basis: Table 3C: average Net Income After Royalties for single-unit franchise owners FY2025 ($46,291, 32% of revenue).

cohorts.basis: Table 4: per-owner average gross revenue FY2025; 20 of 55 owners (36.4%) exceeded the average.

lineItems.notes: Lump sum at signing; non-refundable. Veteran/educator discount of $4,950 available.
Franchisee's own out-of-pocket travel to training location.

lineItems.category: Enhanced Business Coaching and Training Fee
lineItems.notes: Required for first Soccer Stars franchise; paid at signing; applied against coaching visits and calls.
Phone, printer/copier, table and chair if not already owned.

lineItems.category: Starter Kit and Initial Uniforms and Marketing Materials
lineItems.notes: Purchased from an Affiliate before opening.
Off-the-shelf hardware and software if not already owned.
Insurance deposit plus attorney/accountant fees at startup.
Varies by state; $0 if already held.
Working capital for first 3 months including minimum royalties, payroll, advertising, and misc. Does not include owner salary.
$0.13 per person over 400,000 population in Territory; lump sum at signing.
Optional; includes training fee up to $10,000 for travel soccer, Spirit League, and/or Shining Soccer Stars programs.

flatMonthlyFees.name: Recruiting Services and Applicant Tracking System Software

hiddenCosts.name: Grand Opening Marketing Spend
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must spend $5,000 per franchised business on grand opening marketing within the first 12 months.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Conference Fee (per owner)
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory annual conference attendance fee of $600 per owner plus $400 for second attendee.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Conference Travel Expenses
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory conference travel, lodging, and meals estimated at $1,000–$2,000 per person.

hiddenCosts.name: Mandatory Non-Attendance Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $1,000 per owner charged if franchisee fails to attend the annual conference.

hiddenCosts.name: Equipment Replacement (every 2 years)
hiddenCosts.description: Sports equipment must be replaced at least every 2 years; purchased exclusively from an Affiliate.

hiddenCosts.name: Learning Management System
hiddenCosts.description: Annual fee of $50 for the Learning Management System software.

hiddenCosts.name: Merchant Processing Fees (variable)
hiddenCosts.description: Credit card merchant processing fees up to 4% of amounts charged, via Fiserv (exclusive supplier).

hiddenCosts.name: Post-Opening Enhanced Business Coaching (if requested)
hiddenCosts.description: $550 per day per staff member in-person or $80/hour by phone/video for additional coaching after initial period.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $8,750 total transfer fee (split 50/50 between transferor and transferee) if franchisee sells the business.

hiddenCosts.name: Successor Franchise Agreement Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $7,500 fee payable at renewal to sign a new franchise agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Default Notice Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $500 fee charged each time franchisor sends a default notice.

hiddenCosts.name: Audit Costs (if underreporting >2%)
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee pays full audit cost including travel, lodging, wages, and legal fees if underreporting exceeds 2% of gross revenues.

hiddenCosts.name: Plus Program Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time $15,000 fee (all three Plus Programs) or $5,000 (one Plus Program) to add optional travel soccer, Spirit League, or Shining Soccer Stars.

hiddenCosts.name: Transferee Lead Generation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $4,500 commission payable to franchisor if it introduces the buyer who purchases the franchised business.

hiddenCosts.name: Data Security Safeguard Reimbursement
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must reimburse franchisor for all costs of responding to a cyber event caused by the franchisee.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty Payments Regardless of Revenue
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must pay minimum royalties ($700–$800/month) and minimum brand fund contributions ($200/month) even if generating no revenue, with non-payment risking termination.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution (New York)
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be arbitrated in New York, NY, which may increase costs and disadvantage franchisees located in other states.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee's spouse must personally guarantee all financial obligations under the franchise agreement, putting marital assets at risk.

operationalRisks.title: Non-Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee does not receive an exclusive territory and may face competition from other franchisees, company-owned, or affiliate-owned outlets.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Reservation of Rights to Compete
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor and affiliates retain broad rights to operate competing businesses, sell through alternate channels, and use other trademarks within or near the franchisee's territory.

operationalRisks.title: Unregistered Trademark (Shining Soccer Stars)
operationalRisks.description: The 'Shining Soccer Stars' mark is not federally registered; if challenged, franchisee may need to rebrand at their own expense.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Exclusive Supplier Restrictions
operationalRisks.description: Starter kits, uniforms, equipment, and the Back Office Platform must be purchased exclusively from the franchisor's affiliate; franchisee cannot source independently.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Receives Rebates from Suppliers
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor and affiliates receive 2%–5% rebates from designated suppliers (including YAU Sports Store and Fiserv) on franchisee purchases, creating a potential conflict of interest.

operationalRisks.title: Renewal Requires Signing New Agreement with Potentially Different Terms
operationalRisks.description: Renewal requires executing the then-current franchise agreement, which may contain materially different and less favorable terms.

operationalRisks.title: Post-Termination Non-Compete (10-Mile Radius)
operationalRisks.description: After termination or expiration, franchisee and owners are prohibited from operating a similar business within the territory and within 10 miles of any Soccer Stars territory.

operationalRisks.title: Student List Owned by Franchisor
operationalRisks.description: All customer/student lists generated during operation are the sole property of the franchisor and must be surrendered upon termination.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Can Modify Business Model Without Consent
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may change the Confidential Operating Manual, system standards, and required products/services at any time, potentially requiring additional franchisee investment.

operationalRisks.title: Young Franchise System with Limited Track Record
operationalRisks.description: Soccer Stars, LLC was formed in January 2022 and began franchising in August 2022; the system has limited operating history as a franchisor.

operationalRisks.title: Prior Litigation — Franchisee Termination Dispute
operationalRisks.description: A 2025 arbitration (Nurture Nexus Soccer LLC v. Soccer Stars, LLC) over alleged improper termination was settled in February 2026 with Soccer Stars paying $22,616.80.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p. 22 — FDD states franchisees are expected to operate from home; no lease or office space is required, so no rent figure is disclosed.

riskReasons: 3 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $86,300 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $14,156/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise delivers structured developmental soccer and fitness curricula to children at schools, parks, and community venues — a youth enrichment/education service model.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 20–33% — the 40–55% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 1.1–1.7 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 10–18% category band.
Estimated at the 5–12% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–33% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by CohnReznick LLP): Net income of $849K on $3.14M of revenue — roughly 0.7x spent per $1 earned. Losses are widening, not shrinking. Positive net worth of $2.34M. Cash of $23K against $1.27M of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 1.95). Note: about 85% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.1x assets.

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Super Sports Holdings, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
